holdings.name: Vedanta
holdings.reasoning: VEDL is down ~2.7% on June 16 and faces a near-certain passive-selling headwind as MSCI removes it from its Global Standard Indexes effective June 22 — index-tracking funds will be forced sellers. The residual entity post-demerger now reflects only the copper/zinc/residual businesses, and its fundamentals and analyst coverage are still being re-rated; average broker target is ~₹308, but EPS is forecast to fall ~55% in FY27. Short-term direction is close to a coin flip even without the MSCI overhang; with it, near-term risk skews to the downside.

items.name: ICICI Bank
items.why: Consistently improving RoA/RoE, strong retail+SME loan book growth, and well-provisioned balance sheet make it a core private-banking name worth studying
items.risk: Any broad NPA cycle or rate-cut-driven NIM compression could pressure earnings

items.name: Infosys
items.why: Large-cap IT bellwether with a healthy deal-win pipeline, cost-efficiency drive, and steady USD revenue growth — a liquid proxy for India's IT export story
items.risk: Slowdown in US/EU discretionary tech spending and visa/offshoring policy changes remain key headwinds

items.name: Sun Pharmaceutical Industries
items.why: Market-leading domestic formulations franchise plus a growing specialty-US business (Ilumya, Winlevi) diversifies revenue well beyond generics
items.risk: US FDA plant observations or pricing erosion in the US generics market can hurt margins quickly

items.name: Maruti Suzuki India
items.why: Dominant ~40% passenger-vehicle market share, ramp-up of SUV portfolio, and upcoming EV launches position it to capture India's premiumisation trend
items.risk: Yen appreciation raises input costs, and rising EV competition from Tata/Mahindra could erode share over time

items.name: Titan Company
items.why: Branded jewellery (Tanishq) + watches + eyewear give diversified consumer exposure with strong pricing power and a pan-India retail network
items.risk: Gold price spikes compress jewellery margins and can temporarily suppress discretionary demand

items.name: Coal India
items.why: Near-monopoly domestic coal producer with high dividend yield, strong free cash flow, and continued thermal-power demand keeping volumes robust
items.risk: Government price/supply mandates and India's long-term energy-transition away from coal are structural overhangs
